current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.
reasoning: Water is very warm for trout.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water temperatures and moderate turbidity. Recent stocking supports some fishing opportunities with attractor and stocked-trout flies.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is the best window because cooler morning temperatures and moderate sky cover offer the most comfortable feeding conditions for trout despite overall tough conditions.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 81°F, flow is 98.9 cfs, gage height is 3.7 ft, and turbidity is 6.6 NTU at the Etowah River near Dawsonville, GA.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 12.7% in the morning with pressure steady at 30.04 inHg and no rain expected. Wind is light at about 4.6 mph coming from the west with gusts up to 11.5 mph.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will prefer cold-water refuges and oxygenated runs due to warm water stress, and they will position near seams and structure. Use a balanced nymph approach with clean drifts, prioritizing stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell flies. Exercise caution as warm water increases trout stress.
